title: zerna pumpelliana subsp. ornans
intro: Zerna pumpelliana subsp. ornans, also known as the Wild Parsnip, is a flowering plant in the carrot family that is native to North America. The plant contains furanocoumarins, which have been found to have medicinal properties, particularly in their ability to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria and fungi. The health benefits of Wild Parsnip include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve immune function, as well as its use as a natural remedy for digestive issues and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Wild Parsnip can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, and it is also sometimes used as a food ingredient in traditional medicine.
